item.title: PNG and the Failure of EUDR Benchmarking: When “Low Risk” Ignores Reality
item.description: [Act Now PNG] Papua New Guinea’s designation as “low risk” in the EUDR may now be one of the clearest examples of the disconnect with reality.

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts: Independence statement from the President of Bougainville
item.description: [Bougainville News] My fellow Bougainvilleans, In the next few days, the resolve of the 97.8 percent of Bougainvilleans who voted for Bougainville to attain independence will be tested by the tabling of the Bi-Partisan Report on the 2019 Bougainville Referendum in the … Continue reading →

item.title: Eight tips for creating a culture of innovation in Papua New Guinea
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] Peter Williams has been advising businesses on innovation for a long time as part of his role as Chief Edge Officer at Deloitte’s Centre for the Edge in Australia. He shared his top tips for creating a successful culture of innovation at the recent 2026 Innovation PNG Awards in Port Moresby. The pos

item.title: USP climbs more than 400 places in Times Higher Education Sustainability Impact Rankings and secures Top 10 World University Innovation category ranking
item.description: [Education News PNG] The University of the South Pacific (USP) has strengthened its standing among the world’s internationally recognized universities after climbing more than 400 places in the The post USP climbs more than 400 places in Times Higher Education Sustainability Impact Rankings and secures Top 10 World Univ

item.title: PNG Restricts Australian Poultry Imports After Bird Flu Discovery
item.description: [Inside PNG] NAQIA restricts Australian poultry imports after H5N1 bird flu found in WA wild birds. Free-range WA products suspended whilst barn-raised are safe. The post PNG Restricts Australian Poultry Imports After Bird Flu Discovery first appeared on Inside PNG . The post PNG Restricts Australian Poultry Imp

item.title: SP PNG Hunters at the Crossroads as Last-Place Finish Raises Tough Questions Over Direction
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] Commentary by George Lemko The PNG SP Hunters have reached a point where difficult questions can no longer be ignored. The latest Hostplus Cup ladder paints a worrying picture, with the Hunters sitting at the bottom of the 15-team competition after another disappointing campaign. While rival clubs c

item.title: Women in PNG Politics
item.description: [Pawa TV] Papua New Guinea's political history highlights persistent gender inequality, with women facing challenges in attaining parliamentary representation, despite various reform efforts and initiatives aimed at increasing their visibility and participation. The post Women in PNG Politics first appeared o

item.title: The Coolest Vanity Apps for You and Your Girls
item.description: [PNG Buzz] What’s possible in a week? If you dedicated seven days to the achievement of one goal, how ambitious could you make this goal? These were the questions that the multilingual friends Katy and Sara posed themselves when they determined to learn English in one week, to prove that it can be done and any

item.title: PNG Offers Mining Expertise to Help Solomon Islands Build Stronger Resource Sector
item.description: [PNG Facts] Papua New Guinea has offered to help Solomon Islands shape its mining future by sharing decades of experience in resource development while encouraging PNG-owned mining companies to explore investment opportunities across the neighbouring nation. The proposal was raised during talks in Port Moresby

item.title: Lavagirl and Power Meri launch limited-edition women’s rugby league fabric
item.description: [Post Courier] A vibrant new fabric print celebrating women in rugby league launched today at Maku Gifts stores, marking a groundbreaking, first-of-its-kind collaboration between iconic fashion brand Lavagirl and social enterprise Power Meri. The partnership brings together two dynamic, women-led, PNG-focused bran

item.title: Southern region tops Last Man Standing fight
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] By Gabriel Katuho The SOUTHERN region reign victorious as they claim the first place in four regions based on fighter performances. Team NCD and Central province were only two teams representing Southern region in the Last Man Standing Kickboxing quarter finals yesterday, at the Taurama Aquatics cen

item.title: PM Marape pays tribute to Western Province, announces greater resource benefits and push for P’nyang LNG
item.description: [The PNG Sun] Prime Minister James Marape has praised the people of Western Province for sustaining the country’s largest and longest-running resource project for more than four decades, while announcing increased resource benefits for the province and reaffirming the Government’s commitment to progressing the P’

item.title: Mt. Bagana na El Niño givim hevi long Bogenvil
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Maunten paia long Mt. Bagana wantaim drai bilong El Nino i kamapim planti hevi long ol manmeri long Bogenvil. Ol i sot long gutpela wara na kaikai long ples we i stap klostu long we asis bilong maunten i pundaun.

item.title: Sukundimi Walks Before Me nominated for awards
item.description: [Act Now PNG] The striking documentary 'Sukundimi Walks Before Me' has been nominated for both the Documentary Australia Award for Best Documentary and the Sustainable Future Award

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts :Bougainville Landowners demand mining companies halt reopening of copper pit
item.description: [Bougainville News] Traditional landowners are fighting back against plans to reopen an existing Bougainville copper mine which has remained dormant for nearly four decades. During a public event on Bougainville Island last week a warning was declared against the companies who landowners … Continue reading →

item.title: Waste not, feed more: poultry farm’s award-winning solution has wings
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] A small poultry farm in Papua New Guinea’s Central Province has found a sustainable way of reducing its dependence on expensive stock feed. Its solution, which harnesses the power of a humble insect, was one of the big winners in this year’s Innovation PNG Awards. The post Waste not, feed more: poul

item.title: Ten (10) Arrested Including Two (2) Chinese Nationals Over Alleged Murder Of Young PNG Man
item.description: [Inside PNG] Police in Port Moresby have arrested ten suspects over the death of a 20 year old man and the attempted murder of another victim. Among the arrests were four security guards from Fix Security Firm, four shop assistants and two Chinese expatriates from Cash and Carry Shopping Center at Gerehu’s Rainb

item.title: PNG Ready to Share Mining Experience as Solomon Islands Plans Resource Growth
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] Papua New Guinea has pledged to assist Solomon Islands develop a stronger mining industry by sharing its experience in resource governance and encouraging PNG companies to invest in future mining ventures across the neighbouring Melanesian nation. PNG Ready to Share Mining Experience as Solomon Isla

item.title: Tourism in PNG Compared to Fiji
item.description: [Pawa TV] Fiji significantly leads in tourism development over Papua New Guinea, generating USD 2.5 billion compared to PNG's USD 182.94 million, highlighting the need for PNG to improve accessibility and marketing. The post Tourism in PNG Compared to Fiji first appeared on PAWA TV .

item.title: PNG Moves Closer to Rice Self-Sufficiency with Korean Farm Equipment Boost
item.description: [PNG Facts] PNG Moves Closer to Rice Self-Sufficiency with Korean Farm Equipment Boost/Photo supplied Papua New Guinea's drive to cut rice imports has received fresh momentum with the arrival of modern agricultural machinery for the Norikori Rice Project, reinforcing efforts to increase domestic food production

item.title: NCDC enforces HACCP for food businesses
item.description: [Post Courier] National Capital District Commission Council Environmental Health officer Gwen Navara says all Port Moresby food operators must now meet Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points (HACCP) standards, with large-scale businesses required to submit HACCP plans and smaller outlets to provide food safety pl

item.title: North Fly MP condemns drunk driving causing deaths in Kiunga
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] NORTH Fly MP James Donald has strongly condemned drunk driving for causing deaths to innocent lives in the district’s capital, Kiunga, in recent weeks. This comes after two separate incidents which claimed three lives, including a child’s, just within weeks. “Three weeks ago, a mother from Kungim vi

item.title: ICAC strengthens anti-corruption push through business engagement in Lae
item.description: [The PNG Sun] The Independent Commission Against Corruption (ICAC) has stepped up efforts to strengthen integrity, accountability and ethical business practices at the sub-national level, engaging corporate leaders in Lae through a business breakfast focused on governance and anti-corruption partnerships. The eng

item.title: Pisin mekim na balus bungim hevi long Goroka
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Ol pisin i bin pasim ranwe long Goroka ples balus na i givim hevi long wanpela balus bilong PNG Air. Tupela wil bilong balus i bruk tasol no gat manmeri i kisim bagarap long dispela hevi.

item.title: Illegal logging will sink government's timber processing plans
item.description: [Act Now PNG] Government plan to establish four regional centres for timber processing will fail unless it first puts an end to widespread illegal logging

item.title: Bougainville News Alert : Statement from the Office of the President: Response to Chief John L Momis, GCL, former President of Bougainville
item.description: [Bougainville News] It is disappointing to see a leader of John Momis’ stature, a former two term President of Bougainville and longtime Regional Member for Bougainville trying to undermine the good work of the Autonomous Bougainville Government. item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] Dr Ben Hamer, an international expert on the future of work, shares his views on which skills will be most in demand in the economies of the future – and where Papua New Guinea fits into the changing nature of work. item.title: PNG Air Niugini Renews Heart Care Support
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] Air Niugini has renewed its long-standing commitment to helping children with heart disease in Papua New Guinea by signing a Corporate Responsibility Memorandum of Understanding with Operation Open Heart Foundation Inc., reinforcing a partnership that has been saving young lives for more than three

item.title: Early Campaigning in PNG
item.description: [Pawa TV] Concerns grow in Papua New Guinea over early campaigning, which breaches electoral laws. Enforcement is weak, with limited consequences for offenders, raising issues about electoral integrity and public trust. The post Early Campaigning in PNG first appeared on PAWA TV .

item.title: Solomon Airlines Opens New Air Link Between Papua New Guinea and Solomon Islands
item.description: [PNG Facts] A new chapter in regional aviation will begin next month as Solomon Airlines launches scheduled flights linking Papua New Guinea and the Solomon Islands, providing the first direct service between Port Moresby and Honiara under the carrier's latest network expansion. The route, which starts on July

item.title: Attendees raise concerns at Food Safety Workshop
item.description: [Post Courier] Local food business operators and media representatives raised concerns today at the 2026 Food Safety Workshop in Port Moresby, linking issues of unverified Facebook complaints, police involvement in food disputes, and the continued sale of imported products without English labels. item.description: [The PNG Sun] The Central Papua Conference of the Seventh-day Adventist Church today officially launched OneVoice27, a General Conference mission initiative aimed at preparing church members spiritually and equipping them to reach communities ahead of a major global evangelism campaign in 2027. item.description: [Bougainville News] FIVE YEARS ago we farewelled GC Sir Michael Somare with the most deserving tributes around the country. We also paused to remember him as someone whose political life is synonymous with the birth of PNG as a nation state … Continue reading →

item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] SP Brewery’s beers have been sold in PNG since 1952, almost a quarter-century before independence. Now the company is looking to invest in social change alongside business growth opportunities, its Managing Director Ed Weggemans tells Business Advantage PNG . item.title: PNG and Solomon Islands deepen regional partnership with new development agreement

item.title: Cancer Treatment in PNG
item.description: [Pawa TV] Cancer poses a significant health challenge in Papua New Guinea, exacerbated by late diagnoses, limited screening, and inadequate treatment facilities. Strengthening oncology services and prevention efforts is essential to improve outcomes. The post Cancer Treatment in PNG first appeared on PAWA TV

item.title: PNG Capital Port Moresby Faces Extended Power Pressure as Sirinumu Dam Levels Fall Under El Niño Impact
item.description: [PNG Facts] Residents of Port Moresby are being warned to expect continued pressure on electricity supply as PNG Power Ltd scales back water usage at Sirinumu Dam due to worsening drought conditions. The decision comes as rainfall levels remain far below normal across Central Province. PNG Capital Port Moresby

item.title: Agri Food System Transformation in Papua New Guinea
item.description: [Post Courier] A report titled “Agri-Food System Transformation in Papua New Guinea” drawing lessons from the World Bank engagement in the agriculture sector was launched in Lae by the Minister for Agriculture John Boito and World Bank Divisional Director for PNG, Solomon Islands and Vanuatu, Mr Han Fraeters. item.title: Censorship Office Reviews Mid-Year Progress
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] THE Office of Censorship has concluded its 2026 Mid-year Review, bringing together Executive Management and Senior Management to assess the organization’s performance during the first six months of the year and to strengthen its focus on delivering key priorities outlined in the 2026 Annual Operatio

item.title: PM Marape orders nationwide El Niño preparedness as PNG faces risk of prolonged dry season
item.description: [The PNG Sun] PNG Prime Minister James Marape has directed all provinces and districts across the country to begin immediate preparations for a possible prolonged dry season as developing El Niño conditions threaten below-normal rainfall and drought impacts across the country. He made this statement as the debate

item.title: Graun bruk katim Sepik Haiwe
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Bikpela ren i mekim graun i bruk na bagarapim rot long Sepik Haiwe. Dispela rot i save bungim Is na Wes Sepik na i go olgeta long boda bilong PNG wantaim Indonesia.

item.title: PNG Receives First Shipment of HIV Self-Test Kits for National Distribution
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] PNG health authorities have received the first shipment of HIV self-testing kits as part of a nationwide campaign designed to improve testing coverage and strengthen efforts to combat the spread of HIV. The arrival of 26,000 self-screening kits marks the beginning of a broader distribution program t

item.title: The Story of the Meri Blouse
item.description: [Pawa TV] A Symbol of PNG Identity. The meri blouse, locally also called kolos in Tok Pisin, is one of the most recognisable garments associated with women in Papua New Guinea (PNG). Featuring a loose, comfortable fit often crafted from colourful fabric with bold motifs, it serves not just as clothing but as

item.title: PNG signs fresh bilateral cooperation framework with Solomon Islands
item.description: [PNG Facts] A new agreement designed to expand economic and development cooperation between Papua New Guinea and the Solomon Islands has been signed in Port Moresby, setting out a roadmap for collaboration from 2026 through to 2030. The deal followed formal bilateral meetings between Prime Minister James Marape

item.title: CTPM Juniors lifts touch football standards
item.description: [Post Courier] The Corporate Touch Port Moresby (CTPM) competition is reaching its regular-season peak at Murray Barracks Oval One, with the high technical standard on display being heavily credited to a massive surge in junior participation. As Round Two winds down with only a few critical fixtures remaining befo

item.title: PNG Launches Landmark Agriculture Research Framework to Drive Rural Transformation
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] Agriculture Minister Hon. John Boito today launched the National Agriculture Research System (NARS) Policy Framework 2026, a landmark blueprint aimed at modernising agricultural research, strengthening coordination across the sector and delivering practical benefits to farmers throughout the country

item.title: PM Marape announces K5 Million emergency relief for Manus Volcanic Pumice Disaster
item.description: [The PNG Sun] Prime Minister James Marape has announced an immediate K5 million relief package for Manus Province following undersea volcanic activity in the Bismarck Sea that has disrupted coastal and island communities through large deposits of volcanic pumice washing ashore. The emergency support will assist r

item.title: Gavman Minista bam long kar long haiwe
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Minista bilong 'Energy' na Memba bilong Kairuku, Peter Namea Isoaimo i bin bam long kar long 2nd Februeri, 2026 long Hiritano Haiwe. Em i bam long kar wantaim wanpela soldia bilong PNG Defence Force (PNGDF). Ol kar bilong tupela man i stap nau long polis stesin long 15 Mile na wok painimaut i gohet

item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] Queensland has forced the 2026 State of Origin series into a deciding third match after producing a devastating second-half display to defeat New South Wales 44-24 before a record crowd at Melbourne's iconic cricket ground on Wednesday night. Trailing 12-8 at the interval, the Maroons transformed th

item.title: Proposed Polygamy Law in PNG
item.description: [Pawa TV] Polygamy in Papua New Guinea is rooted in tradition, but the government, recognising its impact on gender equality, plans to introduce laws banning it. Consultations will precede this reform. The post Proposed Polygamy Law in PNG first appeared on PAWA TV .

item.description: [The PNG Sun] The Independent Commission Against Corruption (ICAC) says it is considering further legal action following a District Court ruling that dismissed criminal charges against former Kandep MP Don Polye. In a statement released yesterday, ICAC confirmed it had received the ruling delivered by Magistrate

item.description: [Act Now PNG] An international financial crackdown is underway in PNG with global intelligence agency INTERPOL

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts :Rio Tinto urged to accelerate action on remediation of Panguna mine disaster, one year on from investigation
item.description: [Bougainville News] One year on from the release of an independent investigation into Rio Tinto’s former Panguna mine in Bougainville, communities living with the ongoing environmental impact are calling on the company to urgently move towards funding solutions, particularly in areas identified … Continue reading →

item.title: ‘We’re challenging ourselves and our competitors to do better’: Kina Bank’s CEO on the push for innovation in PNG’s finance sector
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] Kina Bank has been leading the way in bringing digital banking products to Papua New Guineans, most recently with the launch of the Pei Beta digital wallet earlier this year. Its CEO Ivan Vidovich shared his bank’s vision on digital payments and innovation in an on-stage interview at the Innovation

item.title: USP Entrepreneurial Fair 2026 brings powerful ideas and youthful energy to Laucala campus
item.description: [Education News PNG] The University of the South Pacific (USP) has launched its 2026 Entrepreneurial Fair, bringing together students, academics, and industry partners under the theme “AI, Innovation and The post USP Entrepreneurial Fair 2026 brings powerful ideas and youthful energy to Laucala campus first appeared on

item.title: PNG Faces High Likelihood of Extraordinary El Niño and Prolonged Drought
item.description: [Inside PNG] PNG braces for an extraordinary dry season with looming water shortages and crop stress. Districts ordered to map local water sources. The post PNG Faces High Likelihood of Extraordinary El Niño and Prolonged Drought first appeared on Inside PNG . The post PNG Faces High Likelihood of Extraordinary

item.title: PNG Court Clears Don Polye of Corruption Charges
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] Former Kandep MP Don Polye has been cleared of three serious criminal allegations after the Waigani District Court dismissed the case against him on Tuesday. The ruling brings an end to a high-profile legal battle that had placed the veteran politician under scrutiny over allegations linked to state

item.title: The Origins of Tok Pisin
item.description: [Pawa TV] Tok Pisin is one of the main languages spoken in Papua New Guinea (PNG) and plays a vital role in communication across the country. With over 800 languages spoken nationwide, Tok Pisin serves as a unifying language that allows people from different regions to understand each other. It is an English-

item.title: Different New Year Celebrations Around the World
item.description: [PNG Buzz] What’s possible in a week? If you dedicated seven days to the achievement of one goal, how ambitious could you make this goal? These were the questions that the multilingual friends Katy and Sara posed themselves when they determined to learn English in one week, to prove that it can be done and any

item.title: PNG's Western Pacific University WPU launches AI-powered digital knowledge hub to expand learning opportunities
item.description: [PNG Facts] Papua New Guinea has strengthened its higher education landscape with the opening of a new AI-powered Digital Knowledge Hub at Western Pacific University, giving students greater access to international learning resources and emerging technologies. Launching the facility in Ialibu, former Prime Mini

item.title: NASFund urges caution over first-time housing advances
item.description: [Post Courier] NASFund Chief Finance Officer Debbie Oli has called for greater awareness of the fund's Housing Advance Scheme while reaffirming NASFund's push for legislative changes to remove restrictions that prevent members from fully accessing their superannuation savings for home ownership. The post NASFund u

item.title: PNG and SI signs Framework Agreement
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] By GINNAH MINI PAPUA New Guinea (PNG) and Solomon Islands (SI) have signed the Framework Agreement on Development and Economic Cooperation (2026-2030), aimed at strengthening cooperation in investment, trade and commerce, mining and resource sectors, fisheries, agriculture, state-owned enterprises,

item.title: Solomon Airlines granted International License to operate Port Moresby-Honiara Route
item.description: [The PNG Sun] The Department of Transport (DOT) has granted Solomon Airlines Limited an International Airline License to operate scheduled international air services between Honiara, Solomon Islands, and Port Moresby, Papua New Guinea, following approval under the Civil Aviation Act 2000 (as amended). The approva

item.title: La Nina bai kam - gavman mas redi
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] National Weather Service (NWS) bilong PNG i tok 'La Nina' bai kam klostu nau tasol i no gat wanpela toksave i kam long gavman yet long wanem samting ol bai mekim o sapos ol i redi long wanem kain ol hevi dispela taim nogut bai nap long bringim.

item.title: Call on Forest Minister and Board to explain failure to cancel FCA permits
item.description: [Act Now PNG] ACT NOW! is calls on the Forest Minster and National Forest Board to explain why controversial FCA logging permits have not been cancelled.

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts : OBEC returns 45 writs, marks completion of 2025 ABG General Election
item.description: [Bougainville News] Bougainville Electoral Commissioner, Desmond Tsianai, today formally returned 45 writs comprising the Presidency, 38 single-member constituencies and the six reserved regional seats for women and Former Combatants. Tsianai formally handed the election writs to the outgoing Speaker, Simon Pentanu, of

item.title: People Moves: Nambawan Super, PNG CORE, Pacific Lime & Cement
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] Executive appointments at Pacific Lime & Cement and Nambawan Super, leadership appointment at PNG CORE. The post People Moves: Nambawan Super, PNG CORE, Pacific Lime & Cement appeared first on Business Advantage PNG .

item.title: USP Pro-Chancellor challenges new generation of Pacific leadersat Emalus graduation
item.description: [Education News PNG] As The University of the South Pacific (USP) graduation season continues across the region, the Emalus Campus ceremony in Vanuatu today served as a powerful The post USP Pro-Chancellor challenges new generation of Pacific leadersat Emalus graduation first appeared on Education News PNG .

item.title: Breadfruit is a Food Often Taken for Granted
item.description: [Inside PNG] NARI urges PNG to prioritize breadfruit as a key food security crop to protect local communities from severe El Niño droughts. The post Breadfruit is a Food Often Taken for Granted first appeared on Inside PNG . The post Breadfruit is a Food Often Taken for Granted appeared first on Inside PNG .

item.title: PNG disaster authorities activate emergency response as El Niño threatens food supplies
item.description: [PNG Facts] Papua New Guinea's National Disaster Centre has activated its emergency response arrangements after the National Weather Service officially declared an El Niño climatic event this month, raising concerns over worsening drought and frost across several parts of the country. The declaration has prompt

item.title: How PNG Realtors are surviving a scarce Housing Market
item.description: [Post Courier] Hausples Expo 2026 highlights why diversification is key to surviving PNG's property market For many visitors at the Hausples Expo 2026, the dream of owning a home remains alive. But behind the displays of new developments and investment opportunities lies a difficult reality facing Papua New Guinea

item.title: Hekari, Tafea draw in opening match
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] Hekari Women FC and Tafea FC have shared the points in an entertaining 2-2 draw to open Group A at OFC Women’s Champions League 2026. In a match filled with numerous twists and turns in momentum, Tafea were pegged back by two goals after taking an early lead, before scoring a stunning second half eq

item.title: PM & NEC Dept moves to electronic payments, ends cheque system for service provider payments
item.description: [The PNG Sun] The Department of Prime Minister and National Executive Council (DPMNEC) has transitioned from cheque payments to an Electronic Funds Transfer (EFT) system, marking a major step towards modernizing government financial operations. Finance Secretary Samuel Penias and Chief Secretary to Government Iva

item.title: Call on Forest Minister and Board to explain failure to cancel FCA permits
item.description: [Act Now PNG] ACT NOW is calling on the Forest Minster and National Forest Board to explain why controversial FCA logging permits have not been cancelled

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts : Could tourism lead Bougainville into the future ?
item.description: [Bougainville News] Bougainville has gone through many turbulent periods over the past 100 years, not least the Civil War of the 1990s, which claimed as many as 20,000 lives. The government is now looking to develop the economy and has its eye … Continue reading →

item.title: High tech and low tech: Innovation PNG 2026 Awards winners announced
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] The winners of Papua New Guinea’s major awards for innovation were announced at a special breakfast on 3 June at the Hilton Hotel in Port Moresby. While two of the awards were built on PNG's growing digital backbone, the third award involved a decidedly offline solution. The post High tech and low t

item.title: USP Concludes Laucala Graduation with a strong focus on Pacific Leadership and Legacies
item.description: [Education News PNG] The University of the South Pacific (USP) concluded its two-day graduation ceremonies at the Laucala Campus in Suva, Fiji, today, marking another important milestone as The post USP Concludes Laucala Graduation with a strong focus on Pacific Leadership and Legacies first appeared on Education News P

item.title: PNG Customs Busts Gold-Smuggling Operation
item.description: [Inside PNG] Nine Chinese nationals arrested in PNG's biggest gold-smuggling bust. 14.5kg of gold sand found on flight PX8. Strict bail granted. The post PNG Customs Busts Gold-Smuggling Operation first appeared on Inside PNG . The post PNG Customs Busts Gold-Smuggling Operation appeared first on Inside PNG .

item.title: PNG Launches Nationwide HIV Self-Testing Program to Expand Access to Care
item.description: [PNG Facts] Papua New Guinea has begun rolling out HIV self-testing kits across the country in a move aimed at increasing testing rates and helping more people learn their HIV status. The National Department of Health announced the program following the arrival of the first batch of self-screening kits that wil

item.title: Highlands and Momase Catholics Unite for Evangelisation workshop in Goroka
item.description: [Post Courier] Over 160 Catholic delegates from Eastern Highlands Province, Simbu, Jiwaka, Mt.Hagen and Madang gathered in Goroka for a regional evangelisation workshop. The event took place at Kefamo Parish from June 22 to 26th, culminating in a town outreach on June 27, 2026, at Goroka. To access this post, you

item.title: Six SHP Fighters take part in Last Man Standing fight
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] By GABRIEL KATUHO Six fighters from Southern Highlands province represented the province in the Last Man Standing quarter finals yesterday at the Taurama Aquatics Centre in Port Moresby. The one-day event hosted by the PNG Kick Boxing Federation brought fighters from across the country who displayed

item.title: Gerehu Secondary School receives over 1’000 books to boost student literacy
item.description: [The PNG Sun] Gerehu Secondary School in Port Moresby has received more than 1,000 new books to strengthen its library resources and support learning for students from Grades 7 to 12. The donation was delivered through a partnership between the University of Papua New Guinea Hagen Urban Students Association and l

item.title: K8 billion lost in illicit forestry activities under investigation
item.description: [Act Now PNG] Estimated K8.78 billion lost through illicit forestry activities and suspected money laundering schemes is now at the centre of four major investigations under Operation Arcadia, a multi-agency crackdown supported by INTERPOL.

item.title: PNG Parliament Holds Final Say on Bougainville Status, Says Marape
item.description: [PNG Facts] Papua New Guinea’s National Parliament remains the body responsible for making the final decision on Bougainville’s political status, Prime Minister James Marape has stated amid renewed discussions on the region’s future. The Prime Minister’s comments come after Bougainville leaders endorsed resolut

item.title: Government fails to answer UN concerns on illegal FCA logging and land abuses
item.description: [Act Now PNG] The Papua New Guinea government has failed to answer questions from the United Nations over the misuse of Forest Clearing Authority (FCA) logging licences and controversial land leases.

item.title: PNG Expands Education Opportunities Through New Facilities and International Links
item.description: [PNG Facts] PNG is continuing efforts to strengthen higher education through investments in student facilities and the pursuit of international partnerships designed to improve academic and technical training. From a new medical student dormitory in Lae to talks with a leading Malaysian university, education au
